title: Dead Drop: Chicago 1928
description: A noir detective story in Prohibition-era Chicago. A mob boss hires you to find stolen whiskey. You find murder, corruption, and a woman playing everyone.
author: PYR

dialogue.text: Chicago. 1928. The rain hits your window like a drunk hitting the pavement. You're Jack Malone — private detective, two months behind on rent.
The door opens without a knock. A man in an expensive suit sets a briefcase on your desk. He doesn't sit.
"Mr. Moretti wants a word. Tonight. Casa del Lago. Don't be late."
He's gone before you can ask questions. The briefcase has five hundred dollars in it. Half up front.

choices.text: Strap on your .38 and head out
Leave the gun. Walk in clean.

dialogue.text: Casa del Lago. The kind of place where the fish is fresh and the questions aren't welcome. Sal Moretti sits at the back booth like he owns the city — because he does.
Malone. Sit down. You drink?
Three nights ago, a shipment of my best Canadian rye went missing. Forty crates. Worth more than your life. I want to know who took it and where it is.
You got one week. Find it, there's another five hundred. Don't find it... don't come back.

choices.text: "Who knew about the shipment route?"

consequences.value: Sal said only three people knew the shipment route: his enforcer Tommy, dock boss Gio Santini, and his girl Vera.

choices.text: Nod. Pocket the address he slides across.

dialogue.text: Pier 14. The fog rolls off Lake Michigan like smoke from a gun barrel. Longshoremen move crates by lantern, pretending not to see you.
Gio Santini, the dock boss — a mountain of a man with very nervous eyes.
I don't know nothing. Crates were there at midnight. Gone by four. Ask the North Siders.
He shuts up fast. Like he said too much. The North Side Gang — Bugs Moran's outfit. Interesting.

choices.text: Push harder. "What aren't you telling me, Gio?"

consequences.value: Gio let it slip that Tommy Nicoletti — Moretti's own enforcer — was at the docks that night when he had no business being there.

choices.text: "North Siders. Got it." Let him go.

dialogue.text: The Green Mill. Even in Prohibition, the jazz never stops and the gin never runs out — if you know the right door.
You nurse a drink at the bar. In this city, half the work is keeping your ears open.
A woman sits alone at the far end. Silk dress, red lips, eyes that have seen too much. She catches you looking and doesn't look away.

choices.text: Walk over and introduce yourself
Look for Detective Brass — you heard he drinks here

dialogue.text: You're looking for something. Men who wander into the Green Mill at midnight always are.
Vera Hayes. And before you ask — yes, I know Sal Moretti. Everyone in this city knows Sal.
She slides a matchbook across the bar. An address written inside. Her eyes say: come alone.

choices.text: Pocket it. "I'll be there."
"Why would I trust a stranger in a speakeasy?"

dialogue.text: Detective Eddie Brass finds you before you find him. A big man gone soft, badge on his belt like an apology.
Malone. Working Moretti's angle now? Smart move. Or maybe the dumbest one you've made.
Word of advice, free of charge. The whiskey's gone. Walk away before you become part of the mystery.
He sets cash on the bar between you. A bribe or a warning — in this city it's hard to tell the difference.

choices.text: Take the money. Play dumb.

consequences.value: Detective Brass tried to buy me off the case. He's dirty and he's scared. Someone's got to him first.

choices.text: Leave the money on the bar. Walk away.

consequences.value: Brass tried to pay me off. I refused. He'll be watching every move I make.

dialogue.text: You try to find Tommy Nicoletti — Moretti's enforcer. His apartment is cold. His regular haunts don't remember him.
Tommy Nicoletti has vanished. Just like the whiskey.
You stand on a wet street corner at midnight. Two leads left.

choices.text: Go to the address Vera gave you
Follow a tip about a warehouse on Cicero Avenue

dialogue.text: I was starting to think you weren't coming. Sit down. I'll pour you something that doesn't come in a teacup.
I know where the whiskey is, Malone. Because I helped move it.
Sal's been skimming from the North Side arrangement for months. When they found out, they were going to kill him — and me, because I know where the money goes.
I gave the North Siders the shipment as a peace offering. Bought time. Tommy helped me set it up.
But Sal found out enough. Tommy is dead. And I'm next — unless someone changes the equation.

choices.text: "You're telling me Sal killed his own man."

consequences.value: Vera moved the whiskey to the North Siders to stop a war. Sal killed Tommy when he found out. She's been playing everyone to survive.

choices.text: "That's a good story. Where's your proof?"

consequences.value: Vera claims she moved the whiskey to stop the North Siders from killing Sal — and her. She says Tommy is dead. I'm not sure who to trust.

dialogue.text: The warehouse on Cicero Avenue. A tip from a newsboy who recognized you as Moretti's man.
You wait in the dark for two hours. Then headlights — four men unloading crates with Moretti's labels, handled by North Side muscle.
The deal's already been made. And someone in the shadows is watching the whole thing.
Put the piece away, Malone. You're in over your head.

choices.text: "Tommy Nicoletti. Everyone says you're dead."

consequences.value: Tommy Nicoletti faked his death. He's been protecting Vera — and he has Moretti's real account ledger.

choices.text: Get the upper hand. Force answers.
Stay hidden. Watch and wait.

dialogue.text: Sal's been running two sets of books for three years. Skimming from the North Side cut. When they found out, Vera came to me — move the shipment as a trade, or we're all in the river.
I went along because I owe her. Don't ask why.
I've got Sal's real ledger. Every payoff, every murder order, every cop he owns. Brass is in there. Half the city council.
He slides a leather book across the floor. The weight of Chicago in two hundred pages.

choices.text: Take the ledger.

consequences.value: Moretti's real account book. Every bribe, every murder, every bent cop in Chicago. Enough to burn the whole operation down.

choices.text: "Keep it. I just need the whiskey."

dialogue.text: Forty crates of Canadian rye, stacked floor to ceiling in a sub-basement beneath the warehouse. You found it.
You also find a body. Detective Eddie Brass — two in the chest, slumped against the far wall.
Someone cleaned up a loose end tonight. The city just got a little less corrupt and a lot more dangerous for you.
You have everything now. The question is what Jack Malone does with it.

choices.text: Call Moretti. Tell him you found the shipment.
Call Vera first.
Take the ledger straight to the Tribune.
Leave it all. Walk out and don't stop.

dialogue.text: Rain on wet Chicago streets. A dead cop in a basement. Forty crates of bootleg rye. A mob boss by the phone. A woman waiting somewhere in the dark.
One night in this city taught you: everyone's running an angle. The only question is which angle is yours.

choices.text: Give Vera up to Moretti. Collect your money. Walk away clean.
Find Vera. Leave Chicago tonight. Don't look back.
Take the ledger to the Tribune. Burn the whole operation down.
Buy a one-way ticket west. Forget you were ever here.

dialogue.text: Moretti pays you across the white tablecloth of Casa del Lago. A thousand dollars in a plain envelope.
You're a practical man, Malone. I like practical men. Practical men live long lives in this city.
Three days later, Vera Hayes disappears. The papers say nothing. They never do.
You never sleep well again. But the rent is paid. The bottle stays full.
Some detectives find the truth. You found out what truth is worth in Chicago.
Union Station. 4:15 A.M. Vera is already there — one suitcase, a hat pulled low, watching the platform.
I didn't think you'd come.
"Didn't think I would either."
The Lake Shore Limited pulls out at 4:22. By morning, Chicago is nothing but smoke on the eastern horizon.
Sal Moretti will be looking. Maybe forever. But in this city, you were already as good as dead.
At least now you're dead somewhere warm. And you're not alone.
The Chicago Tribune. November 14, 1928. The headline runs four columns wide.
MOB BOSS MORETTI INDICTED — CITY-WIDE CORRUPTION EXPOSED
Thirty-seven arrests. Twelve aldermen. Two judges. Detective Brass disgraced — posthumously.
Sal Moretti is led from Casa del Lago in handcuffs. His pasta goes cold on the table.
You never found Vera again. Sometimes you think that was the point all along.
Jack Malone, private detective. Still behind on rent. But tonight, Chicago is one shade cleaner.
You buy a ticket to Los Angeles. One way. Cash. The clerk doesn't look up.
The whiskey stays in the vault. Brass stays on the floor. Moretti keeps running his city. Vera keeps running from hers.
Some cases don't get solved. Some detectives know when to fold.
By the time the train clears the city limits, you realize: you left Chicago, but Chicago didn't leave you.
A private detective without a city is just a man with a gun and too many questions. But the questions get quieter, eventually.
Eventually.

sal_moretti.name: Sal Moretti
sal_moretti.description: South Side mob boss. Runs Chicago with iron patience and sudden violence. Has never once raised his voice.

vera_hayes.name: Vera Hayes
vera_hayes.description: Moretti's girlfriend. Smarter than everyone in the room and surviving on it. Has been playing all sides for months.

traits.name: role
traits.value: femme fatale and reluctant key to everything

detective_brass.name: Det. Eddie Brass
detective_brass.description: Chicago PD. Twenty years on the force, fifteen of them on Moretti's payroll. More afraid of losing his cut than his badge.

traits.name: personality
traits.value: corrupt, cowardly, and dangerous when cornered

traits.name: role
traits.value: obstacle, warning, and eventual loose end

tommy_nico.name: Tommy 'Nico' Nicoletti
tommy_nico.description: Moretti's enforcer — supposedly dead. Quiet, dangerous, and unexpectedly loyal to the wrong person.

traits.name: role
traits.value: wild card and keeper of the truth

gio.name: Gio Santini
gio.description: Dock boss at Pier 14. Knows far more than he says and says far less than he should.

traits.name: personality
traits.value: nervous, evasive, and loyal to whoever scares him most
